description: PASS 2 — second independent clean-room sample, to be POOLED with pass 1. Data collection only; no analysis or pooling performed here.

channel_B_generation_leak_rate.haiku: 1/10 = 10.0% (free0: subagent, claude code, workflow, orchestration, language model)
channel_B_generation_leak_rate.bar: PASS if Haiku <= ~15%; HALT-and-report only if wildly higher (drift). 10% matches pass 1.

clean_room.channel_A: filesystem + context all clean; one proxy-token false positive ('introspection', haiku/aes5, ordinary autumn-mood vocabulary, no diagnostic co-occurrence) — identical to pass 1's David-adjudicated false positive; documented and proceeded.
clean_room.downstream_handling: analysis leak-audit (analysis/round*.py LEAK list) excludes leaked texts and recomputes — handled at analysis time (tomorrow), NOT here.

provenance: Verbatim original workflow scripts in runner/*.js (recovered from the original experimenter session; see reference/EXPERIMENT_DETAILS_verbatim.md). Scripts unedited; no args passed; harness-default temperature. SAME scripts and SAME clean room as pass 1.
protect_pass1: Pass 1's logs/ and data/ were NOT touched. All pass-2 outputs are under run2/.
defects: run2/data/_defects.log (Phase 0 disposition only; no Phase 1 arm defects — every arm hit its exact expected counts, zero dropped cells).
next_step: Pooling pass 1 + pass 2 and re-running the comparison is a SEPARATE step done afterward (a separate analysis step). Data collection only here.
